hypothesis_en: Mr President, Mr President of the Council, Mr President of the Commission, ladies and gentlemen, I do not want to begin my speech without thanking, in my capacity as a Spaniard and as President of the Committee on Public Liberties, the European Council for its support of this Parliament's proposal to celebrate 11 March as European Day in Memory of the Victims of Terrorism. As we have unfortunately been for some time in my country, the civilized world is in danger of a new enemy whose limits are not defined: terrorism, and the fight against this enemy, equipped with many tentacles and who kills and murders in a ruthless and brutal way, is not a war to use, it is not a traditional war, but it is, and there is no doubt about it, an unequal and criminal war against structured societies. The European Union must unite all its efforts and urgently put in place all the legal, financial and human instruments to combat terrorism, and that is why I believe that this Parliament is obliged to give the European Council a message of support for the measures it adopted on 25 March in its declaration against terrorism, but also to demand it with the legitimacy that the We can no longer accept the lack of political will in the implementation of these measures by the Member States, as is the case today, and I will only highlight some of the more obvious examples, such as the Euro-Order or the Framework Decision on terrorism. On the other hand, ladies and gentlemen, reality shows that the European Union is often involved in major difficulties in decision-making, and with 25 Member States, I fear that this will de facto lead to the blocking of many decisions. I consider, ladies and gentlemen, and I conclude, that this would greatly facilitate our work against what has proved to be the greatest current danger to the stability of democratic and rule-based societies.
reference_en: President (1.07) President in Office of Council distinguished colleagues I wish to begin by on behalf of the Spanish people and as Chairman of the (1.15) Committee of Citizens Freedoms I'd like to thank you all for the proposal to use the eleventh of March as a day in memory of the victims of terrorism these most recent events have caused consternation and pain but they have also made us aware as unfortunately we have been for some time in my country that the civilised world is under threat there's a danger we are facing a new enemy whose confines are unknown this is terrorism terrorism has many arms many limbs many tentacles and it's not a traditional form of warfare but it is no doubt war it's a criminal and unfair kind of war on our citizens and on their freedoms and on democracy and the European Union should galvanise all of its forces pull all of its forces so that we can combat terrorism together and it's for these reasons that I believe that the European Parliament must give the Council a message of support for the measures adopted on the twenty-fifth of March and must but must also require of Council as representatives of democracy in Europe that we cannot accept (1.57) the lack of transposal the lack of logic in transposal of certain measures by certain Member States for example the Euro warrant and we cannot certainly agz= accept budgetary or procedural uses reasons being used to block certain progress for example when it comes to the anti-terrorist unit that was set up within Europol after the eleventh of September furthermore distinguished colleagues reality has shown that the European Union is facing major difficulties when it comes to making decisions already and when there are twenty-five Member States I think this is going to lead to blockage on many fronts so why not follow the example of the latest European Council that used the Solidarity claus= that= Clause that's in the draft of the Constitutional Treaty and why not anticipate the procedure whereby qualified majority can be used for making decisions I think Mr President that this would make for great progress against what has shown itself to be the greatest threat to our deme= democratic societies and the rule of law no more and this is this is no less than what our citizens deserve thank you President
source_es: gracias señor Presidente señor Presidente del Consejo señor Presidente de la Comisión Señorías no quiero comenzar mi intervención sin agradecer en mi calidad de español y como presidente de la Comisión de Libertades Públicas el apoyo que ha recibido por parte del Consejo Europeo la propuesta hecha por este Parlamento de celebrar el día once de marzo el día europeo en memoria de las víctimas del terrorismo los últimos acontecimientos además de habernos sumido en el desconcierto y el dolor nos han hecho conscientes como desgraciadamente lo somos desde hace tiempo en mi país de que el mundo civilizado está en riesgo en peligro ante un nuevo enemigo cuyos límites no están definidos el terrorismo y la lucha contra este enemigo provisto de muchos tentáculos y que mata y asesina de una forma despiadada y brutal no es una guerra al uso no es una guerra tradicional pero sí es y de ello no cabe la menor duda una guerra desigual y criminal contra las sociedades estructuradas sobre principios como la libertad y como la democracia la Unión Europea debe unir todos sus esfuerzos y poner en marcha con urgencia todos los instrumentos jurídicos financieros y humanos para combatir el terrorismo es por todo ello por lo que considero que este Parlamento está obligado a darle al Consejo Europeo un mensaje de respaldo a las medidas que adoptó el pasado veinticinco de marzo contenidas en su declaración contra el terrorismo pero también a exigirle con la legitimidad que nos da el representar a los ciudadanos europeos que ni un minuto más podemos aceptar la falta de voluntad política en la implementación de estas medidas por los Estados miembros como ocurre en la actualidad y destaco solo algunos ejemplos más evidentes como la Euroorden o la Decisión marco sobre terrorismo y menos aún podemos aceptar que por razones puramente presupuestarias o de procedimiento se hayan desmantelado instrumentos que podrían ser tan eficaces en la lucha contra el terrorismo como es el caso de la Unidad antiterrorista creada en el seno de Europol a raíz de los atentados del once de septiembre por otra parte Señorías la realidad demuestra que la Unión Europea se ve a menudo envuelta en importantes dificultades a la hora de adoptar decisiones y ello con veinticinco Estados miembros me temo que se traducirá de facto en el bloqueo de muchas decisiones por qué no entonces y siguiendo el ejemplo del último Consejo Europeo que ha utilizado la cláusula de solidaridad prevista en el aún proyecto de Tratado constitucional anticipar la puesta en marcha del sistema de la adopción de toma de decisiones por mayoría cualificada ya incluido en el texto de nuestra futura Constitución considero Señorías y termino que ello facilitaría mucho nuestra labor contra el que ha demostrado ser el mayor peligro actual para la estabilidad de las sociedades democráticas y basadas en el estado de derecho no es menos Señorías lo que me= se merecen nuestros ciudadanos muchas gracias señor Presidente
